title: Sabah Borneo Boat Rental: Costs, Boats and When to Go in 2026
html:
Chartering a boat in Sabah Borneo offers a unique way to explore the Sulu Sea and its islands. A typical half-day motorboat charter from Jesselton Point in Kota Kinabalu costs around €250 to €400. To find your ideal vessel you can rent a boat through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace connecting renters with verified owners.

Where to set off in Sabah Borneo

Most charters depart from Kota Kinabalu, the state capital. The main marina is Sutera Harbour Marina, which offers full facilities and easy access to the Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park. Jesselton Point is the main public jetty for island-hopping trips to Sapi, Manukan, and Gaya islands. Further north, the Kudat area provides a gateway to the Tip of Borneo and the Balambangan archipelago. For more remote expeditions, Semporna town is the launchpad for Sipadan and Mabul islands, known for world-class diving. Each departure point offers a different perspective of the region's marine landscapes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sabah Borneo?

Costs vary widely by boat type and duration, but expect to pay from €80 for a shared tour to over €2,000 for a crewed yacht. Here is a typical price breakdown in EUR:

  • Shared island-hopping tour: €80 to €100 per person for a half-day trip to the Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park
  • Small motorboat half-day: €250 to €450 for up to 6 guests, including basic safety gear
  • Catamaran full-day: €800 to €1,200 for up to 12 guests, with snorkelling equipment and lunch
  • Crewed yacht full-day: €1,500 to €2,500 for up to 10 guests, with skipper, crew, and premium services

Fuel costs are usually included in private charters, but confirm when booking. BoatHire24 listings show all-inclusive prices so you can compare easily.

Which boat to rent

Choose your boat based on group size, budget, and whether you want speed or comfort. The options below cover the most popular types for the area.

  1. Speedboat, Best for small groups who want to reach islands quickly, up to 6 guests, from €250 for half a day. An example is this speedboat, the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open with 150 HP.
  2. Catamaran, Ideal for stable cruising, snorkelling trips, and larger parties, up to 12 guests, from €800 for a full day.
  3. Yacht or Motor Yacht, For luxury day charters or overnight stays, up to 10 guests and from €1,500 for a full day with crew.
  4. Fishing Boat, Purpose-built for sport fishing in the Sulu Sea, up to 6 guests with local guide, from €400 for half a day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sabah Borneo?

For bareboat rentals (self-drive), you typically need a valid powerboat licence or an ICC; a skippered charter requires no licence. Boat rental operators in Sabah generally require proof of experience for self-drive charters. For most visitors, hiring a skipper is simpler and recommended for unfamiliar waters. The operator will confirm exact requirements before you book. Always check the specific listing on BoatHire24 for licence details.

Local tip: the best time to charter is between March and October when the sea is calm and the wind is light. Avoid November to February when the northeast monsoon can bring rougher conditions, especially around Kudat and the northern coast.

At a glance

  • Price range: €80 to €2,500 per trip
  • Peak months: April to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours to 8 hours (full day)
  • Typical capacity: 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: Euro quoted, but local payment in Malaysian Ringgit (MYR) is common

Questions about boat rental in Sabah Borneo

Can you rent a boat without a licence in Sabah Borneo?

Yes, you can hire a skippered charter which includes a captain, so no licence is needed for the renter.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Sabah Borneo?

Charter companies provide safety briefings and life jackets; always choose a verified operator through BoatHire24 for peace of mind.

What is the best island to visit by boat from Kota Kinabalu?

Gaya Island is the largest and offers mangroves and snorkelling, while Sapi Island is popular for its beaches and short crossing from Jesselton Point.

How far is the Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park from Kota Kinabalu?

The park is only 3 to 5 nautical miles from the city, making it a quick 15-20 minute boat ride.
